courses.title: Indemnitor Basics
courses.subtitle: Understanding Your Role as a Co-Signer
courses.audience: First-time co-signers, family members of defendants
courses.description: Everything you need to know before co-signing a bail bond. Learn what liability means, understand the lifecycle of a bond from arrest to case closure, and know your rights and obligations as an indemnitor in the state of Florida.
courses.longDescription: When a loved one is arrested, the process can feel overwhelming. This free course walks you through every step — from understanding what a bail bond actually is, to knowing exactly what you're agreeing to when you co-sign. We cover Florida-specific laws, your financial obligations, what happens if the defendant misses court, and how to protect yourself throughout the process. By the end, you'll have the confidence to make informed decisions during one of life's most stressful moments.

modules.title: What Is a Bail Bond?
modules.description: The basics of bail, bonds, and how the Florida court system sets bail amounts.

modules.title: What Does It Mean to Co-Sign?
modules.description: Your legal obligations as an indemnitor — liability, collateral, and financial responsibility.

modules.title: The Lifecycle of a Bond
modules.description: Follow a bond from arrest to booking, bail hearing, release, court appearances, and case closure.

modules.title: Court Dates & Failure to Appear
modules.description: What happens when a defendant misses court — warrants, bond forfeiture, and your exposure.

modules.title: Your Rights as an Indemnitor
modules.description: How to surrender a defendant, request bond cancellation, and protect your collateral.

modules.title: Payment Plans & Financial Obligations
modules.description: Understanding the 10% premium, payment plan terms, and what happens if payments are missed.

courses.outcome: Verified Co-Signer status in your Shamrock client profile
requirements: Valid email address for certificate delivery

courses.title: The Agent Path
courses.subtitle: Become a Licensed Florida Bail Bond Agent
courses.audience: Aspiring bail bond agents seeking Florida licensure
courses.description: Comprehensive training program covering everything you need to become a licensed Limited Surety Agent in Florida. Covers FL Statutes 648 and 903, premium calculations, risk assessment, ethics, and hands-on technology training with the Shamrock Portal.
courses.longDescription: This is the real deal — the full path from civilian to licensed bail bond agent in the state of Florida. Our hybrid program combines classroom instruction with hands-on experience, covering every aspect of the bail bond industry. You'll master Florida's legal framework (Statutes 648 and 903), learn premium calculations, understand risk assessment algorithms, and get certified on the technology that powers Shamrock's statewide operations. Upon completion, you'll be prepared for the state exam and eligible for a 1-year internship placement at Shamrock Bail Bonds.

modules.title: Florida Statutes 648 & 903
modules.description: The complete legal framework governing bail bonds in Florida — licensing requirements, agent obligations, and regulatory compliance.

modules.title: Bail Bond Premium Calculations
modules.description: Master the $100/charge minimum, 10% rule, transfer fees, and county-specific pricing strategies.

modules.title: Risk Assessment & Underwriting
modules.description: Learn Shamrock's 0–100 risk scoring algorithm, flight risk indicators, and collateral evaluation techniques.

modules.title: Ethics & DFS Compliance
modules.description: Florida Department of Financial Services compliance requirements, ethical obligations, and professional conduct standards.

modules.title: Criminal Justice Procedures
modules.description: Understanding arrest, booking, arraignment, bond hearings, and the court system across Florida's 20 judicial circuits.

modules.title: Underwriting & Collateral Evaluation
modules.description: Assessing indemnitor financial stability, collateral types and valuation, and managing agency risk exposure.

modules.title: Skip Tracing Fundamentals
modules.description: Basic investigation techniques, database searches, and defendant location strategies within legal boundaries.

modules.title: Technology: The Shamrock Portal
modules.description: Hands-on training with SignNow digital paperwork, the intake pipeline, CRM tools, and mobile-first client management.

modules.title: Court Procedures & Bond Forfeiture Prevention
modules.description: Managing court dates, forfeiture timelines, motion to set aside, and estreature defense strategies.

modules.title: State Exam Preparation
modules.description: Comprehensive review sessions, practice tests, study guides, and test-taking strategies for the FL DFS exam.

modules.title: 1-Year Internship Requirements
modules.description: Understanding the temporary license period, supervision requirements, and the path to full licensure.

modules.title: Building Your Career
modules.description: From temporary to full license — business development, client acquisition, agency partnerships, and career advancement.

courses.outcome: State exam readiness + internship placement opportunity at Shamrock Bail Bonds
requirements: Must be at least 18 years old
Valid Florida driver's license or state ID
courses.certificateTitle: Shamrock Bail Bonds — Agent Path Completion Certificate

courses.title: Risk Management & Skip Tracing
courses.subtitle: Advanced Techniques for Industry Professionals
courses.audience: Licensed agents, recovery professionals, agency owners
courses.description: Advanced seminar covering TLO/IRB cross-referencing, digital footprint tracking, county scraper intelligence, and modern skip tracing techniques used by Shamrock's technology-driven recovery operations.
courses.longDescription: This isn't your typical CE course. This advanced seminar pulls back the curtain on the technology and investigative techniques that power Shamrock's statewide operations. You'll learn how we use automated county jail scrapers to detect new arrests in real-time, cross-reference databases like TLO and IRB for deep background analysis, track digital footprints across social media, and manage GPS/check-in monitoring systems. This is the future of the bail bond industry — and you'll learn it here first.

modules.title: Advanced TLO/IRB Cross-Referencing
modules.description: Deep-dive into professional background investigation databases — search strategies, red flag identification, and report interpretation.

modules.title: Digital Footprint Analysis & OSINT
modules.description: Open-source intelligence techniques for locating defendants — social media analysis, public records, and digital trail mapping.

modules.title: County Scraper Intelligence
modules.description: How Shamrock's 'Scout' system automatically monitors jail rosters across 7+ Florida counties to detect new arrests and generate leads.

modules.title: Social Media Investigation
modules.description: Legal and ethical frameworks for social media monitoring, geo-tag analysis, and behavioral pattern recognition.

modules.title: GPS & Check-In Systems
modules.description: Administering defendant monitoring programs — GPS tracking, selfie verification, compliance check-ins, and violation protocols.

modules.title: Defendant Risk Re-Evaluation
modules.description: Dynamic risk scoring — when and how to escalate cases, revoke bonds, or recommend surrender based on changing circumstances.

modules.title: Bond Forfeiture Prevention & Recovery
modules.description: Proactive strategies to prevent forfeiture — court reminder systems, defendant engagement, and recovery action plans.

modules.title: Legal Boundaries
modules.description: What you can and can't do — Florida statutes on bail recovery, privacy laws, trespassing, and use of force limitations.

courses.outcome: Advanced Risk Management certification from Shamrock Bail Bonds
requirements: Active Florida bail bond agent license (or equivalent)
Minimum 1 year of industry experience recommended
courses.certificateTitle: Shamrock Bail Bonds — Advanced Risk Management

faqs.question: What is Shamrock Bail School?
faqs.answer: Shamrock Bail School is the educational division of Shamrock Bail Bonds, LLC — Florida's most technology-driven bail bond agency. We offer courses ranging from free co-signer education to comprehensive professional agent training programs, all designed to bring transparency and professionalism to the bail bond industry.

faqs.question: Do I need any prior experience to take a course?
faqs.answer: No! Our Indemnitor Basics course (Track A) is designed for complete beginners — anyone who needs to understand the bail bond process. The Agent Path (Track B) requires a high school diploma but no prior industry experience. Only the Risk Management seminar (Track C) is designed for existing industry professionals.

faqs.question: How much does the 120-hour pre-licensing course cost?
faqs.answer: Contact us at (239) 332-2245 for current pricing on our Agent Path program. We offer competitive rates and may have financing options available for qualified applicants.

faqs.question: Is the Indemnitor Basics course really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, 100% free. We believe that educated co-signers make better decisions and have smoother experiences. When you understand the process, everyone benefits — you, your loved one, and the justice system.

faqs.question: Do I get a certificate after completing a course?
faqs.answer: Yes! All three tracks include a digital certificate of completion. The Indemnitor Basics course awards a 'Verified Co-Signer' status. The Agent Path provides a completion certificate recognized for state exam preparation. The Risk Management seminar awards an Advanced Certification.

faqs.question: Can I take courses from anywhere in Florida?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our Indemnitor Basics course is fully online and on-demand — take it from anywhere, anytime. The Agent Path is a hybrid program with both online and in-person components at our Fort Myers location. The Risk Management seminar is available both in-person and via live Zoom.

faqs.question: Will completing the Agent Path guarantee me a job?
faqs.answer: While we cannot guarantee employment, graduates of the Agent Path program are eligible for internship placement consideration at Shamrock Bail Bonds. Our program is specifically designed to prepare you for both the state exam and real-world agency operations.

faqs.question: How do I become a licensed bail bondsman in Florida?
faqs.answer: You must: (1) Be at least 18 and a US citizen, (2) Complete a state-approved 120-hour pre-licensing course, (3) Pass the Florida DFS state exam, (4) Clear a Level 2 FDLE background check, and (5) Complete a 1-year supervised internship under a licensed agency. Our Agent Path program covers steps 1-4 and can facilitate step 5.
